headlineSummary: Google Gemini drew the clearest positive reaction thanks to a comment praising Deep Research as previously infeasible and “a quantum-leap” in capability, while OpenAI and Anthropic were in a close tie on a neutral access-related mention [E1][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic had a neutral, limited-evidence mention in a speculative question about whether it could reduce afternoon congestion, and it was in a close tie with OpenAI [E3].
OpenAI had a neutral, limited-evidence mention in the same congestion question, and it was in a close tie with Anthropic [E3].
Microsoft Copilot had a mildly positive mention for a useful “steer with message” feature that the commenter says they use often [E2].

Google Gemini received the strongest positive signal, with a commenter crediting Gemini Deep Research for producing investigative reports they could not previously do and calling the experience a quantum leap [E1].

evidence.excerpt: But today I’ve already had Gemini Deep Research write investigative reports previously unfeasible for me due to time and expertise constraints, such as the possible timeline for robotics replacing all physical human labor given real world constraints: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LiahX2deGBoAb7kqIi5zNZm0... (spoiler alert, not for a long time yet due to extremely limited critical supply chain constraints.) When I read that report, I realized we had quantum-leaped from Sci-Fi to reality in just a few years.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Gemini Deep Research and strongly praises its capability as enabling previously infeasible investigative reports, calling the experience a leap from sci-fi to reality.

evidence.excerpt: GitHub Copilot has a "steer with message" feature where I can course correct mid execution. I use it often.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly references GitHub Copilot and describes a useful steering feature they use often, indicating mild praise for the product.

evidence.excerpt: So will this help openai/anthropic have lower congestion in the afternoons if they implement something similar?

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names OpenAI in a speculative question about reducing service congestion; it points to access/availability concerns without clearly praising or criticizing the provider.
The commenter explicitly names Anthropic in the same speculative question about reducing service congestion; it concerns availability but does not express a clear evaluative stance.
